question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file whose filename is fewer than 10 characters long, causing `streamFileData.getFilename().substring(0, 10)` to throw a `StringIndexOutOfBoundsException`, crashing the importer and preventing legitimate transactions from being archived or processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a filename whose first 10 characters resolve to a path-traversal sequence (e.g., `../../../`) so that `dataPath.resolve(STREAMS).resolve(filename.substring(0, 10))` writes archived transaction data outside the intended directory, corrupting or overwriting legitimate transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a stream filename whose first 10 characters are all identical (e.g., `0000000000`) so that all tampered transaction files are archived into the same day-bucket folder, overwriting legitimate transaction archives and causing data integrity loss?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted `network` value containing the `NETWORK_PREFIX_DELIMITER` character (`-`) such that `StringUtils.substringBefore(this.network, NETWORK_PREFIX_DELIMITER)` returns an empty string, causing the importer to resolve an incorrect or empty network name and process tampered transaction streams from an unintended source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNetworkPrefix()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `network` value with a delimiter-only string (e.g., `-`) so that `getNetworkPrefix()` returns an empty string instead of null, causing downstream components that rely on a non-null prefix to skip signature or hash verification for submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions via a network identifier that, after `substringBefore` and `toLowerCase()` normalization, matches `demo`, causing `HederaNetwork.isAllowAnonymousAccess()` to return `true` and bypassing authentication checks on submitted transaction streams?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: HederaNetwork.isAllowAnonymousAccess()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `isAllowAnonymousAccess` returns `true` only for `DEMO` network to inject tampered transactions into a demo-network importer instance without any credential, since no authentication is enforced for that network path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: HederaNetwork.getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where `getBucketName()` returns an empty string (for `OTHER` network) by manipulating the `network` property, causing the importer to read transaction stream files from an attacker-controlled or undefined bucket, allowing tampered transactions to be ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user force the `network` value to resolve to `OTHER` so that `getBlockStreamBucketName()` returns an empty string, causing the block stream downloader to fall back to an unvalidated or attacker-controlled source and ingest tampered block transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: HederaNetwork.hasCutover()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `network` property so that `hasCutover()` returns `false` for a production network, causing the importer to skip the block-stream cutover logic and continue processing legacy record-stream files that may contain tampered transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: HederaNetwork.hasCutover()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `network` value that, after `substringBefore` normalization, does not equal `mainnet` or `testnet`, causing `hasCutover()` to return `false` and disabling TSS/block-proof verification for submitted transactions, allowing tampered transactions to pass without proper cryptographic validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: consensusMode)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfiguration where `consensusMode` is set to `EQUAL` (all nodes equally weighted) to reduce the effective stake threshold required for consensus, allowing a minority of colluding nodes to reach consensus on tampered transaction stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: consensusMode)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `ConsensusMode.STAKE` (which uses all nodes' stake, not just address-book nodes) to include rogue nodes with artificially high stake values in the consensus calculation, enabling tampered transaction files to reach the consensus threshold?
